Blueprint Report #2
The 2012 “Traffic Black Book” Marketing Funnel
Reminder: These Blueprint Reports alternate every other month with
issues of the Marketing Funnels Uncensored Letter - 6 times a year a new
Blueprint Report, 6 times a year a new issue of the Letter.

-- 1 of 58 --

Since early 2012, Chad Hamzeh’s Traffic Black Book (TBB) has gone on to
become one of the more popular traffic generation training programs online.
The product, itself, has gone through at least one major renovation. As has the
main marketing funnel.
In this month’s Blueprint Report, we’ll be taking a deep, intimate look at the 2012
version of the TBB marketing funnel - the one that really put Traffic Black Book
on the map.
Side note: I personally found Traffic Black Book to be a solid course. Kudos
to Chad for doing a fantastic job of articulating his method of media-
buying and traffic generation, as well as assembling some savvy traffic
experts and contributors.
With that being said, my primary go-to resource for all things traffic is
Marketing Funnel Automation Coaching Client, Charles Kirkland.
The dude is a true mad scientist when it comes to generating gobs of paid
traffic. Not too mention, a super-generous, ethical marketer and all around
good guy. You can learn more from him at
www.MediaBuyerAssociation.com.
My second go-to resource for media buying and traffic generation is
Jonathan Mizel and his Traffic Evolution Program.
Jonathan is truly one of the godfathers of internet marketing. And has
more experience with traffic generation and media buying than any other
single person I know. He’s also a solid dude. You can learn more from
Jonathan by going to www.TrafficEvolution.com.
*Neither of those links are affiliate links. Nor do I stand to make any
money by recommending either of them.
We’re going to start off by talking about the main marketing funnel that took TBB
from a virtual unknown resource to a highly-respected, wildly popular training
program.
I’ll be pointing out lots of the little nuances behind different parts of this funnel.
So, I highly recommend you get a pad and pen, and get prepared to take some
serious notes.
Marketing Funnels Uncensored - Blueprint Report #2
Copyright 2013 - MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 2 of 58 --

9 Main Marketing Funnel Components:
1. Squeeze Page
2. Sales Letter
3. Exit Downsell 1
4. Exit Downsell 2
5. Email Follow-Up Sequence
6. Upsell 1
7. Alternate Upsell 1
8. Upsell 2
9. Order Form
Component #1: Squeeze Page
EXHIBIT #1 shows an example of one of the squeeze pages used.
There’s nothing revolutionary or extraordinary here. If you’ve been around the
internet marketing game for any length of time, you’ve seen more pages like this
than you can probably count.
There are just a few things I want to point out.
First, take a look at the “warning” pre-head. There are really three main ways to
use this type of pre-head: (1) as a real warning of true impending danger (i.e.
coming stock collapse, increase in disease, etc.), (2) as a qualification process for
weeding out prospects that don’t really fall into your target audience, or (3) as an
attention-grabber that sets the frame for what the prospect is about to read.
The third approach is what Chad did. If you read the pre-head you’ll notice that
even though it’s a “warning”, it really contains a promise of benefit, “...make
immediate and life changing income from home.”
On the surface, it appears that it’s qualifying visitors and attempting to weed out
certain prospects. But, it’s really a “buried promise” that appeals to virtually every
online marketer. And, in the process, conveys the idea (sets the frame) that what
the prospect is about to read has the power to generate “immediate and life
changing income”.
Second, notice how the main headline immediately features the free video in red.
When you’re offer is for something free don’t bury it down in the copy or hide it
somewhere. Make sure it’s loud and clear right up front.
Marketing Funnels Uncensored - Blueprint Report #2
Copyright 2013 - MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 3 of 58 --

Third, notice how the income numbers in the headline and sub-headline are
specific. Not vague, rounded, or approximates. In marketing, specificity leads to
greater believability.
Specificity sells. Vague generalities do not.
The only number that could have been more specific is the time frame it took to
generate the income (i.e. “...in under 12 months”). I would have put the specific
number of months or weeks.
Fourth, notice how the main headline features a fairly big income number,
“$375,420.36”. While the sub-head includes a smaller daily income number,
“$1,336.37”.
What this does is help to ensure the squeeze page offer appeals to both newbie
marketers as well as those more experienced.
While the bigger income claim may intimidate newbie marketers, the smaller
number allows for a temporary “suspension of disbelief” and the possibility of
results. It’s both exciting and reasonably doable for the newbie marketer.
Fifth, notice how the sub-headline also covers two common traffic generation
objections from newbies - no website and no product. This is a common
technique seen in the form of - “How To Get XYZ Without Needing ABC”. The
“without” portion being the exact thing or things prospect don’t have or don’t
want.
Sixth, and lastly, notice how the optin form asks only for email address and refers
to it as “Best Email”. In almost every test we’ve conducted, just asking for email
address produces a higher optin rate than asking for email address and first
name. As well, framing the request with “Best Email” or “Primary Email Address”
tends to get a slightly higher rate of better quality email addresses, rather than
the throw-away addresses used by many.
Now, one important note before we move on from the squeeze page.
As of the writing of this Blueprint Report (late 2013), I do not believe this type of
squeeze page appeal would work nearly as well today as it did in 2012.
The reason?
The overall market sophistication level, as it relates to media buying, is much
higher than it was a year and a half to two years ago.
Marketing Funnels Uncensored - Blueprint Report #2
Copyright 2013 - MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 4 of 58 --

There have been a lot more media buying training products introduced into the
marketplace. And, therefore, as I teach my Marketing Funnel Automation
Coaching Clients, what’s needed today to cut through the clutter and get and keep
attention is a Big Idea behind the squeeze page offer. Something that instantly
conveys: this is new, this is different, this is unique, this fresh, etc.
Component #2: Sales Letter
EXHIBIT #2 is a screenshot of one of the earliest headline variations used for
the sales letter.
EXHIBIT #3 is screenshot of the entire sales letter that functioned as the
primary control for the 2012 TBB marketing funnel. What you can not see in the
screenshot is an on-camera video of Chad right at the top of the letter above the
download now button.
I want you to notice a just a couple of key things within this letter:
First is the preponderance of up-front proof in the first one-third of the letter.
Since the letter leads with a big, bold income claim, lots of proof is needed to
legitimize the headline. And the more proof, the better.
Now, a quick aside:
Having a preponderance of proof is one of the most critical elements of any
marketing funnel. Legendary advertising copywriter, Gary Bencivenga, has
routinely said that the backbone of every high converting marketing piece is
proof. And my friend, Jeff Walker, teaches his Product Launch Formula clients
that without proof there is no launch.
Inside of the MFA Coaching Program, clients routinely hear me talk about the
necessity of having at least one proof element for every single promise and
statement of benefit made throughout your marketing funnel.
Without proof, any benefit you present or promise you make is nothing more
than a claim. Proof is what legitimizes it and makes it credible and believable to
prospects.
While most marketers typically rely solely on testimonials and screenshots for
proof, you want to mix up your proof elements using a variety and preponderance
of proof points. Again, the more proof the better.
Marketing Funnels Uncensored - Blueprint Report #2
Copyright 2013 - MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 5 of 58 --

Just remember: testimonials and screenshots are proof points. So they should be
placed strategically throughout your marketing funnel to PROVE the legitimacy
of a particular claim, benefit, or promise.
Don’t just place testimonials and screen-shots willy-nilly throughout your
marketing funnel or throughout your sales letter (or VSL). Place them
strategically at targeted points so they prove something you’ve claimed.
Make sense?
Good.
Onward.
The second thing I want you to notice is how the sales letter is driven by story.
Hopefully, you don’t need me to tell you about the power of story in marketing.
Stories are, arguably, the single most powerful method for presenting any
marketing message. They not only garner more attention and engagement than a
straight sales or marketing message, they allow us as marketers to make certain
claims, demonstrate certain benefits and outcomes, and convey certain rewards...
implicitly.
Meaning: stories allow us to communicate certain ideas within the confines of the
story, without having to come right out and say them explicitly.
Implicit messages are almost always received with a lot less resistance (if any at
all) compared to explicit, straight, in-your-face sales and marketing messages.
In this example, Chad has done a terrific job of conveying his experience within a
story in a way that resonates with the market. And he’s primarily done so in the
form of one of the most powerful archetypes - the Hero’s Journey.
In case you’re... dare I say... not familiar with the Hero’s Journey, its typical
scaled-down structure within marketing looks something like this:
Stage 1: This is what my life looked like before I discovered XYZ
(Association with Prospect).
Stage 2: This is everything I tried to fix my problem... without success
(Introduction of the conflict).
Marketing Funnels Uncensored - Blueprint Report #2
Copyright 2013 - MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 6 of 58 --

Stage 3: This is how, when, and where I discovered XYZ (Introduction of
the “special method”).
Stage 4: These are the results XYZ gave me (Results the prospect wants).
Stage 5: This is my life now, after having discovered and used XYZ (Life the
prospects wants).
Now, just in case you’re a total marketing nerd like me, here’s the “official” full
breakdown of the 12 steps of the Hero’s Journey as presented and shared by
Joseph Campbell.
See which of your favorites movies can fit directly into these 12 steps.
1. 	Ordinary world
2. Call to adventure
3. Refusal of the call
4. Meeting the mentor
5. 	Crossing the threshold
6. Tests, allies, enemies
7. 	Approach to the inmost cave
8. Ordeal
9. Reward (Seizing the sword)
10.The road back
11. Resurrection
12. Return with elixir
Okay, back to the sales letter.
The last thing I want to point out is the price.
In this version, the price for Traffic Black Book is just $47.
In your typical marketing funnel, the front-end product tends to be lower priced
to decrease the barrier of entry for new customers. 	This is not always the case.
But, being that the goal of the front-end (for savvy direct response marketers) is
the acquisition of maximum new customers, a lower price point tends to perform
best.
Component #3: Exit Downsell Offer 1
In the event prospects didn’t take advantage of the offer, upon exit they would be
redirected to a simple discounted downsell offer. Not worth showing here.
Marketing Funnels Uncensored - Blueprint Report #2
Copyright 2013 - MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 7 of 58 --

In this case the offer was a simple $10 discount off the normal $47 price, bringing
the price down to just $37.
The only point I will make here about discounted downsell offers... is.... justify
the discount.
Give a logical, real reason why... a justification for why... the offer is discounted.
The reason can be a bonus that’s not included, a component of the product not
included, etc.
Without a justification or reason for the discount, the offer devalues your
product, brings in to question your integrity on pricing, and makes prospects
wonder the pricing on your other products is the lowest available. All not good.
Component #4: Exit Downsell Offer 2
If prospects did not take advantage of the discount offer, upon exit they were
redirected once again to another alternative downsell offer.
This time the offer is for a $1 trial for Traffic Black Book with the remaining $36
paid after 14-days.
EXHIBIT #4 shows the offer.
Component #5: Email Follow-Up Sequence
This is the only component of the TBB marketing funnel I felt was really weak. It
didn’t have any of the elements of what a solid email follow-up sequence should
have. Hence, the reason for not including any of the emails here. Not worth
spending time on.
A good email follow-up sequence should pick up with where the story started
and/or was shared at the start of the funnel (i.e. squeeze page and/or sales offer),
and should have a series of connected, sequential messages that build in intensity
over time.
Often, when a marketing funnel lacks a solid, strategic follow-up sequence,
marketers are forced to pull out all “downsell stops” right up front. This certainly
helps in converting some of the prospects who wouldn’t have otherwise bought.
However, with a solid follow-up sequence you can convert a nice percentage of
those prospects into full pay buyers, rather than simply discount buyers.
Marketing Funnels Uncensored - Blueprint Report #2
Copyright 2013 - MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 8 of 58 --

In other words, with a good quality follow-up sequence you can convert a lot
more prospects into full pay buyers, then at the end of sequence introduce the
first discount offer with follow-up, and finally the second $1 discount offer, also
with follow-up.
This allows you to maximize the percentage of full pay buyers, before dropping
your pants and presenting them with the discount and $1 trial.
Component #6: Upsell 1
Once a prospect completes the order form (which I’ll be covering in just a bit)
they were presented with the first upsell offer.
It was for something called the Traffic Black Book "X" Edition.
The price point: $197.
Here’s a snippet of how Chad described it:
“Look over my shoulder and see my LIVE campaigns. Watch me set them up
from scratch and force them into profitability. Includes REAL examples of
landing pages and entire campaigns you can simply "copy and paste".”
What makes this such a great upsell offer after the main Traffic Black Book
course is how congruent it is with the main product. It takes what new customers
will be learning and gives them the opportunity to see exactly how Chad does it.
This is similar to a copywriting course having an upsell where customers can
watch the copywriter actually write copy, test copy, tweak copy, etc. Or, a course
on picking-up women having an upsell where customers can watch the pick-up
artist actually applying and using the techniques dynamically in different
situations.
As well, “look over my shoulder” type of products, coming from experts, tend to
have lots of appeal to new customers. It gives them more confidence in reaping
rewards since it has a sense of “simply follow along with what I do”.
Plus, it gives the coveted “behind the scenes view” so many customers want...
thinking there’s some magic going on that they don’t know about.
As well, what makes this a great upsell after the main Traffic Black Book product
is that it doesn’t do or say anything that would make the new customer feel like
something was left out of the main product.
Marketing Funnels Uncensored - Blueprint Report #2
Copyright 2013 - MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 9 of 58 --

Nope.
They got it all.
This upsell just gives them the opportunity to see how Chad personally executes
on everything they’re going to learn inside the main product. Good stuff!
Component #7 & #8: Alternate Upsell 1 & Upsell 2
If the prospect did not take advantage of the first upsell, they were presented with
an alternative upsell offer.
The offer was for a training program called CPA Truth.
The price point was $197.
It was presented as the gold standard of CPA courses with a stated value of
$1997.
Ironically, this is the same offer that is presented if they do not take the first
upsell offer as well. In other words, this is the offer that is presented to customers
whether they take the first upsell offer or not.
Not sure why Chad did it this way. Regardless, I feel it’s the first of two major
shortcomings with the upsell/downsell sequence.
The second shortcoming being the price point of the offer as the alternate to
upsell 1.
A $197 price point is totally fine when a customer just purchased a $97 product.
However, I’ve never seen an alternate upsell offer, priced higher than the original
upsell offer, convert well.
I would have either kept the alternate upsell offer at the same price point as the
original upsell offer - $97. Or, I would have lowered the price point to between
$47 - $67 and offered the transcripts and slides from the videos.
Side note: One thing a lot of marketers miss is the point of the upsell/
downsell sequence of a front-end marketing funnel.
Remember, the objective of a front-end customer acquisition marketing
funnel is to acquire maximum new customers at break even or better. All
Marketing Funnels Uncensored - Blueprint Report #2
Copyright 2013 - MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 10 of 58 --

additional transactions and revenue generated via the upsell/downsell
sequence is still considered the front-end.
The goal of a front-end marketing funnel’s upsell/downsell sequence is not
only to help improve the overall economics of the funnel by increasing the
average front-end transaction size, it’s also to increase the transaction
value of as many new customers as possible.
This is why I would rather have 100 new customers take a $10 upsell offer,
than just one new customer take a $1000 upsell offer.
Same economics, but 100 better quality customers instead of just one.
Component #9: Order Form
EXHIBIT #5 shows the order form from the current TBB marketing funnel.
I chose to include this order form, instead of the original, because there are a few
really savvy things being done here (not on the original) I want you to make note
of.
First, very quickly, notice how the copy at the top of order form is written in the
customer’s voice.
Notice how it highlights an additional big promise right in the first sentence,
“...get my own traffic supply on demand”. This is something done, and common,
with all of the best direct response order forms.
Second, you’ll also notice the money-back guarantee is mentioned right in that
initial paragraph.
And, if you review the rest of the order form, you’ll notice the guarantee is stated
an additional four times. That’s a total of five times the money back guarantee is
mentioned on one order form.
Third, also very common, notice how the price is given below the package value.
This creates the perspective in the prospect’s mind that they’re getting a deal.
And everybody loves to get a deal.
As well look at the wording for the price - “Today’s Special Price”. This creates
some slight urgency since, even though it’s not said, the prospect doesn’t know if
or when the price will change, go up, etc.
Marketing Funnels Uncensored - Blueprint Report #2
Copyright 2013 - MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 11 of 58 --

Thus far, out of everything I’ve mentioned, there’s nothing revolutionary or
uncommon being done on this order form. All common, solid direct response
marketing principles at play for an order form.
Now let’s get into some of the really savvy stuff at play.
Number one is the additional Bump Offer for the continuity program in the
square box below the price. There are three things going on here I want you to
grasp.
First is how this is the first mention of this program or offer anywhere. It wasn’t
mentioned prior to this spot. Here, it’s being presented to prospects ready to buy
at the point of sale. A true bump offer.
Second, look at how the headline for this offer is stated in the negative, “If this
box is unchecked I will NOT receive my FREE 14 Day Trial to Traffic Black Book
Masterclass – an exclusive monthly ADVANCED training program.”
And, with the checkbox being checked by default, it requires the prospect to
proactively deny the offer, if they don’t want it, and with physical action
acknowledge they are choosing to pass on the offer.
All of our testing data shows you will get a much higher “take rate” with your
bump offer on your order forms if they require prospects to uncheck the box to
deny the offer, rather than check the box to accept the offer.
Third, the “5 Reasons We’re Different From Other "Traffic" Programs” is very
well done. It’s not often used. But, recent test results (I’ve seen) have shown a
decrease in shopping cart abandonment when it is.
If you read the copy in this section, you’ll see how at least four of the five reasons
where translated into benefits for the prospect. Not just reasons/features,
benefits. Three of which were new and not previously discussed. A sort of...
piling-on of new benefits, if you will.
Lastly, if you read over the FAQ section you’ll see how each of the questions
proposed and answered are really there to overcome common objections/
concerns (i.e. money needed, speed of results, time commitment, etc.). And, of
course, another place to highlight that it’s a money-back guaranteed risk-free
proposition.
Now, in an ideal world, this page would be the actual order form instead of just a
bridge page prior to the order form. My guess is it was done this way since sales
Marketing Funnels Uncensored - Blueprint Report #2
Copyright 2013 - MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 12 of 58 --

were processed via Clickbank. And CB doesn’t provide the opportunity for bump
offers right on their order forms.
One final point about order forms in general:
A good order form can significantly reduce shopping cart abandonment, and
therefore quite significantly impact overall conversions and ROI.
This is why you want to make sure you go the distance when assembling your
order form. You want to be sure to put together the most powerful order form
possible.
Don’t ever view or approach your order form as an after-thought or simple
formality in the sales process.
It’s far more important than that.
This is why many top copywriters actually begin by creating the order form first.
A good order form should almost be able to stand on its own as a sales piece.
It should convey the big promise.
It should hit on the main benefits. It should overcome key objections and
concerns. And it should clearly lay-out exactly what the prospect gets and what
they need to do to get it (the offer).
In some cases (as demonstrated in our last Blueprint Report), the order form can
also present an additional bonus or additional discount for fast response or larger
purchase. It can present an additional, bolder money-back guarantee (often
conditional). It can present additional proof points. And it can even present a
deadline or additional reason for response today.
Overall, Chad has done a nice job with this order form. There’s a lot to be learned
and gleaned from EXHIBIT #5.
BONUS Component: Affiliate Recruitment Page
As you know, I believe the reliance on affiliates and joint venture partners has
been more responsible for the downfall and vulnerability of more online
businesses than possibly any other single strategy.
But, that in no way means I’m not a believer in the value of affiliates and joint
ventures.
Marketing Funnels Uncensored - Blueprint Report #2
Copyright 2013 - MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 13 of 58 --

Not at all.
In fact, I’m a big believer in the enormous value of both... when they’re used
correctly - as a non-essential add-on method of acquiring new customers.
In other words: affiliates and joint ventures are a fantastic additional way of
getting new customers... after you’ve engineered, tested, and proven you have a
viable front-end marketing funnel that can be used to acquire new customers
from paid media at break-even or better.
The essential foundational component of your business is the front-end funnel
that converts prospects from cold, paid traffic. With a front-end funnel like this in
place, your business is not vulnerable to whims and flakiness of affiliates or joint
venture partners.
With all of that being said, EXHIBIT #6 shows the headline used on the top of
the partner recruitment page for TBB.
The best partner recruitment pages are designed similar to sales pages. They’re
designed to “sell” partners on promoting the offer.
Chad did a good job of this.
Here were the key elements included on the rest of the page:
Prospective partners were shown a breakdown of the funnel (i.e. main offer,
upsells, downsells, exit offers, etc.).
A list of respected “already signed-up” partners was shown (for credibility, social
proof, implied endorsement, and association).
Testimonials, from partners who had already promoted, were given.
Some conversion data was given (5% conversions from organic traffic with 70%
of buyers taking at least one upsell offer).
A couple of proof elements demonstrating EPC’s being experienced were shared
(from $2.26 - $4.50).
And, of course, the affiliate commission structure for each part of the marketing
funnel was given - a straight 60% commission on every offer.
Marketing Funnels Uncensored - Blueprint Report #2
Copyright 2013 - MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 14 of 58 --

Sidenote: How you structure your partner commissions for the front-end
funnel is totally dependent on your main objective.
If you’re solely interested in new customer acquisition, commissions should
be a lot higher. I’ve seen rates as high as 100%+ on the entire front-end
funnel.
What this does is give partners the opportunity to bank a lot more money
from you than they could from competitors, making the idea of promoting
your offer a lot more appealing.
And, if you have a solid backend in place, with a well-designed backend
funnel, even with paying out 100%+ on the front-end, you know you’ll be
extracting profit with your backend process. (This is how the big-dogs in
direct response leverage lifetime customer value to look at the investment
they’re willing to make on the front-end for acquisition.)
For the marketers that don’t have a solid backend in place, for whatever
reason, partner commissions need to be lower, allowing for the marketer
to generate some cash and profits on the front-end funnel.
Wrap-Up
Alright, so there you have it... another marketing funnel breakdown, dissection,
and analysis.
Next Blueprint Report, you’ll see the antithesis of this funnel. You’ll see a front-
end marketing funnel that is driven by an amazing follow-up process.
You’ll see the strategy behind the overall follow-up process, as well as the strategy
behind each of the steps that make up the follow-up sequence.
Lastly, I want to leave you with this reminder...
The stability and consistency of your business rests on the back of your front-end
marketing funnel.
The most critical and essential element of your business is the ability to
consistently and reliably acquire new customers at break even or better from
scalable traffic generation sources and channels (paid media).
This is why, when engineering and re-engineering your front-end marketing
funnel, you must go the distance. Don’t ever underestimate the task at hand!
Marketing Funnels Uncensored - Blueprint Report #2
Copyright 2013 - MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 15 of 58 --

Traffic Black Book

-- 20 of 58 --

From: Chad Hamzeh
Re: Gargantuan Traffic. On Demand
Dear Frustrated Friend,
If you're racking your brains trying to figure out why you're STILL not seeing the kind of success you dream about on
the internet...
Glue your eyes to every word on this webpage.
I'm going to reveal the rock solid wall standing between you and the BIG money online...
And show you how to BLAST through it
with an UNLIMITED traffic supply...
Traffic Black Book
4 of 33

-- 21 of 58 --

Because accessing a limitless traffic source…
Driving a relentless flood of rabid, targeted traffic to ANY webpage you choose…
Is exactly how I produce paydays of $2,744.00 and $3,650.00 and more—on demand. And in a moment, I'll show you
how to tap into biggest, fastest, most wide-open traffic sources on the 'net.
… and force it to produce paydays of $2,744.00 and $3,650.00 and more.
First, reality check…
If you still believe in ‘push button’ money machines and one-click magic bullet software…
… or that fat, bearded strangers in red jumpsuits drop down chimneys at Christmas…
… this page probably isn’t for you.
But if you’re sick and tired of being on the other side of that wall… feeling shut out from where the REAL money is
being made…
Then you owe it to yourself to read every word on this page.
Because I know better than most what it’s like to struggle… how it feels to find yourself drowning in debt… and what
it means to finally taste sweet, six-figure success…
But if you saw the broke and disillusioned state I was in last year…
You'd never guess that right
now I'd be stuffing 5-figure
monthly paychecks into my pockets
like clockwork...
You see, I ran as fast as I could away from the 9 to 5.
There, I was chained to a cubicle carrying out brain-numbing tasks and dealing with idiot bossholes day after day.
Does that sound familiar?
One day, I plain had enough.
And in a wild bid for freedom, I slammed my notice down on my boss's desk and jetted off with my wife to Thailand.
For good.
But, as you'll soon see, disaster was just around the corner.
Our dreams of spending the rest of our days in paradise were about to come crashing down around our ears.
I hit financial rock bottom... maxed out credit cards and spiraled into debt...
Suddenly that crappy cubicle job was starting to look a lot more secure…
Fast forward 12 months later?

weather!

We thought the online money would roll in pretty quickly.
I imagined tapping away at my keyboard for an hour in the morning… then heading off to the beach to spend the rest
of the day with my wife.

We were living in paradise.

boys really doing?"

Frequently Asked Questions:
Q: How much cash do I need to get started?
A: If you want to see REAL cash results quickly, I always recommend you begin with at least $500. That’s
enough to see tangible results early on (which you can then put back into growing your profits).
Q: How fast can I make $100 a day?
A: It’s very hard for me to tell on an indiviual case how fast you’ll pick-up the training. But from my own
experiences teaching this program, I’ve seen many people reach $1000 a day within their first 2 months.
Q: How many hours a week do I need to commit?
A: The steps you need to take are exactly the same whether you condense them into a week... or string them out
over 6 months :)
Q: The course seems a little overwhelming... Is there a way I can “fast track”
my results?
A: Absolutely. In Module 6 we recommend you focus on ONE aspect of paid traffic – media buys, networks,
facebook or Google. Pick just one. Following the videos step by step will then help you get the results you want
much faster than trying to do everything at once.
Q. What if I don’t like the program, can I return it for a refund?
A: Yes, very easily. In the miniscule chance the program doesn’t bring you the results you want for your business,
you can request a full refund. Just email me at info@trafficblackbook.com
